detail.title: Heist
detail.overview: Millions in stolen cash. Missing luxury bourbon. Watch ordinary people almost get away with these extraordinary heists in this true crime series.

videos.name: Heist | Official First Look Clip | Netflix

trailer.name: Heist | Official First Look Clip | Netflix

recommendations.title: Homicide Hunter: Lt Joe Kenda
recommendations.overview: A non-fiction investigative series of murder cases told through the personal experience of retired detective, Lieutenant Joe Kenda. Through re-enactments, discussions with investigation teams, and interviews with victims' families and other involved persons, the show highlights Kenda's successes with his 400 homicide case history and 92 percent solution rate.

recommendations.title: Trust Me: The False Prophet
recommendations.overview: In this true-crime documentary, a cult expert and filmmaker infiltrate a polygamist sect to expose a self-proclaimed prophet and bring him to justice.

recommendations.title: Conversations with a Killer: The Jeffrey Dahmer Tapes
recommendations.overview: Serial killer Jeffrey Dahmer confesses to his gruesome crimes in unguarded interviews, offering an unsettling view into a disturbed mind.

recommendations.title: The Diamond Heist
recommendations.overview: This stranger-than-fiction crime caper follows the attempted robbery of a precious gem, told by the gangsters who did it and the police on their tail.

recommendations.title: See No Evil
recommendations.overview: Every second of every day, millions of Americans are caught on CCTV. Living in a surveillance society means everyday actions are caught on camera, mostly of honest citizens going about their daily lives.  But a few are guilty of unspeakable crimes. Video doesn't discriminate; criminals also end up on film. See no Evil is a groundbreaking series that presents dramatic stories about how real crimes are solved with the aid of surveillance cameras. Police reveal how CCTV footage has unlocked the answer to cases that otherwise might have remained unsolved- leaving dangerous killers at large. The series features real footage and dramatic reconstruction, combined with first-hand testimony from police, witnesses, and families.

recommendations.title: Crime Scene: The Texas Killing Fields
recommendations.overview: An overgrown field and a stretch of highway connect a series of grisly murders spanning several decades as grieving families search for answers.

recommendations.title: I AM A KILLER
recommendations.overview: Death row inmates convicted of capital murder give a firsthand account of their crimes in this documentary series.

recommendations.title: American Manhunt: The Boston Marathon Bombing
recommendations.overview: Archive footage, chilling reenactments and exclusive interviews shed new light on a tragedy — and two brothers — that suspended a city in terror.

recommendations.title: No One Saw a Thing
recommendations.overview: A six-part, true crime docuseries about a mysterious murder of Ken Rex McElro that took place in the ‘80s with at least 60 witnesses present, all of whom deny seeing anything.

recommendations.title: D.B. Cooper: Where Are You?!
recommendations.overview: In 1971, a skyjacker parachutes off a plane with a bag of stolen cash — and gets away with it. Decades later, his identity remains a compelling mystery.

recommendations.title: The Innocence Files
recommendations.overview: The personal stories behind eight cases of wrongful conviction that the Innocence Project and organizations within the Innocence Network have worked to highlight and overturn.

recommendations.title: The Pharmacist
recommendations.overview: After his son's tragic death, a Louisiana pharmacist goes to extremes to expose the rampant corruption behind the opioid addiction crisis.

recommendations.title: Evil Genius
recommendations.overview: This baffling true crime story starts with the grisly death of a pizza man who robs a bank with a bomb around his neck - and gets weirder from there.

recommendations.title: Killer Sally
recommendations.overview: Interviews with friends, family and Sally McNeil herself chart a bodybuilding couple’s rocky marriage — and its shocking end in a Valentine's Day murder.

recommendations.title: Lorena
recommendations.overview: This four-part docuseries investigates the events of 1993, where Lorena Bobbitt sliced off her husband's penis after years of abuse. John and Lorena Bobbitt's stories exploded into a 24-hour news cycle. She became a national joke, her suffering ignored by the male-dominated press. But as John spiraled downward, Lorena found strength in the scars of her ordeal.

recommendations.title: The Jinx
recommendations.overview: Robert Durst, scion of NY's billionaire real-estate family, has been accused of three murders but has evaded justice for over 30 years. Durst speaks in this true crime series, revealing secrets of a case that has baffled authorities.

recommendations.title: Catching Killers
recommendations.overview: The investigators behind infamous serial killer cases reveal the harrowing, chilling details of their extraordinary efforts in this true crime series.

recommendations.title: Homicide
recommendations.overview: Detectives and prosecutors revisit their most challenging homicide cases in this chilling true-crime docuseries.

recommendations.title: Inside the World's Toughest Prisons
recommendations.overview: Crime journalist Raphael Rowe goes behind the bars of some of the world’s most notorious and toughest prisons. Immersing himself in maximum security facilities around the world to live as a prisoner, he encounters the inmates locked up for their crimes and meets the men and women on the right side of law tasked with keeping the criminals behind bars.

recommendations.title: Medal of Honor
recommendations.overview: Honoring service members whose courage merited the awarding of a Medal of Honor, this docudrama series re-creates their inspiring true stories.

similar.title: Braqueurs
similar.overview: Making use of re-enactments with the help of crime specialists and journalists, as well as the testimonies of victims, each episode sheds light on the modus operandi of thieves who have shaken up the people of Quebec, then and now.

similar.title: Dateline
similar.overview: Dateline NBC, or simply Dateline, is a weekly American television newsmagazine series. It was previously the network's flagship newsmagazine, but now focuses mainly on true crime stories with only occasional editions that focus on other topics.

similar.title: Unsolved Mysteries
similar.overview: Combines four to five segments of dramatic re-enactments, interviews and updates of real human and paranormal mysteries. An audience interactive call-to-action request allowed viewers to call in with tips to help solve the cases.

similar.title: No One Saw a Thing
similar.overview: A six-part, true crime docuseries about a mysterious murder of Ken Rex McElro that took place in the ‘80s with at least 60 witnesses present, all of whom deny seeing anything.

similar.title: Keeper of the Ashes: The Oklahoma Girl Scout Murders
similar.overview: The true story of three young Oklahoma girls, found murdered after their first night at sleep-away camp. The tragedy, as well as the manhunt and trial of their suspected killer, captivated the nation in the summer of 1977. But decades later, uncertainties surrounding the case continue to haunt the Tulsa community, local law enforcement, and the victims' families.

similar.title: Pandora's Box: Unleashing Evil
similar.overview: Most stories end with the crime, but sometimes the crime scene represents just the tip of the iceberg. From a murder scene that tipped police off to a polygamist secret society to a random house fire that revealed family secrets that had been buried for decades, Pandora's Box: Unleashing Evil uncovers gripping investigations that get more sordid as every piece of evidence is examined.

similar.title: Meet, Marry, Murder
similar.overview: What happens when the one you commit to spend your life with ends up taking it instead? This series looks at partner-homicide cases; stories of wives killing husbands, husbands killing wives (much more common), and love turning to loathing as passions get out of control.

similar.title: The Booze, Bets and Sex That Built America
similar.overview: Divulges surprising origin stories of the American alcohol, gambling, sex, and tobacco industries and the ambitiously notorious entrepreneurs who built some of history's biggest fortunes on the nation's cravings.

similar.title: Love Con Revenge
similar.overview: Victims of romance scams try to reclaim their lives with help from "The Tinder Swindler" target Cecilie Fjellhøy and private investigator Brianne Joseph.

similar.title: This Is a Robbery: The World's Biggest Art Heist
similar.overview: In 1990, two men dressed as cops con their way into a Boston museum and steal a fortune in art. Take a deep dive into this daring and notorious crime.

similar.title: What The Killer Did Next
similar.overview: Ian Stewart murdered his partner, children's author Helen Bailey, in 2016. He dumped her and her dog in a septic tank at her Hertfordshire home, but reported her

similar.title: The Wonderland Massacre & the Secret History of Hollywood
similar.overview: Michael Connelly takes viewers on a wild journey into the twisted story of the massacre at Wonderland Avenue on July 1, 1981 – one of Hollywood's most famous murder cases that has fascinated people for more than 40 years.

similar.title: What Lies Beneath
similar.overview: Gripping real-life cases of some gruesome murder trials, featuring genuine CCTV footage as well as shocking courtroom video recordings used to piece together the truth and find the killer.

similar.title: Two Shallow Graves
similar.overview: The disappearance of the McStay's haunts police for three years until their remains turn up in the desert. Prosecutors say evidence proves Chase Merritt killed them, Merritt said he's innocence, claiming they are misinterpreting the facts.

similar.title: Keep Sweet: Pray and Obey
similar.overview: In interviews and rare home video footage, ex-FLDS members share the truth about their isolated community — and the events that pushed them to leave.

similar.title: Murdaugh Murders: Deadly Dynasty
similar.overview: The powerful Murdaugh family has held sway over the South Carolina “low country” for a century—until now. Alleged murder, corruption, and deception are revealed. Meanwhile Alex Murdaugh, 4th generation of the legal dynasty, stands accused of enough financial crimes to sentence him to more than 700 years in prison…and the saga continues.

similar.title: Desperately Seeking Soulmate: Escaping Twin Flames Universe
similar.overview: A deep-dive into the world of Twin Flames Universe, an online group run out of suburban Michigan by the captivating YouTube influencers Jeff and Shaleia Divine. The couple's promise is to match members with their perfect partner, but former participants describe the extreme lengths they have taken in order to claim their "twin flame." Journalist Alice Hines soon discovers that the promise of true love may come at a price.

similar.title: Devil in the Web
similar.overview: Internet crimes often take a devastating toll on the victims. Strangers can now access others' personal lives to harass, violate, and even physically attack them. Once this type of crime occurs, it's nearly impossible to get justice -- or relief.

similar.title: Killer Ratings
similar.overview: Brazilian TV personality and politician Wallace Souza faces accusations of masterminding the violent crimes he reported on and rallied against.

similar.title: Murder for Hire
similar.overview: A series that dives into the dark world of contract killings, showcasing some of the most fascinating murder for hire cases ever caught on tape.
